blocks.content: The formal statement took forty minutes. Julian gave it the same way he gave everything — clean, unembellished, no speculation inserted where observation ended. When it was done Davenport turned off the recorder.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Off record. The phone call records — we pulled them this morning.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: I haven't told you anything about them.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: You went quiet when I mentioned them earlier. You go quiet when something is confirmed, not when something is unknown. So the records told you something you weren't expecting. And you're not sharing it because you're still deciding what it means.
Davenport stared at him.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Is it always like this. Talking to you.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I'm told it gets easier.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Does it?

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: No.
Davenport almost smiled. First time.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: The records are an active part of the investigation.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Of course. But when you're ready to tell me — and you will be, because you're going to hit something you can't read — I'll be next door.
He stood. Picked up his jacket. Headed for the door.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Mr. Crane.
Julian turned.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: You said the beginning was months ago. That someone planned this.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: How do you know it wasn't years.
Julian looked at him steadily. Something shifted — just slightly — behind his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Good. You're thinking.
